project_summary: A comprehensive online examination platform with built-in proctoring, role-aware exam delivery, collaborative teacher assignment, live exam/question refresh, folder-based question banks, permission-aligned user management, entity-aware soft-delete/restore capabilities, and analytics-rich dashboards. Features RBAC (Admin, Examiner, Student), exam creation with MCQ/Subjective questions, student + teacher assignment controls, timed attempts with auto-save and live question sync, browser-based proctoring (tab switch/blur detection), automatic MCQ grading, role-scoped Recently Deleted recovery, and shared student/teacher/admin reporting views. Built with FastAPI (async Python) backend, PostgreSQL database, and a Vite-compiled TypeScript + vanilla CSS frontend served via Nginx. Orchestrated with Docker Compose.

architecture.pattern: Layered Architecture (Presentation → API → Service → Repository → Database)
architecture.state_management: Server-side session via JWT tokens; client-side localStorage for token persistence
architecture.notes: Services and repositories directories still exist but remain unused — business logic lives directly in API route handlers. Frontend remains a 4-page architecture, and the dashboard now aligns with backend RBAC for examiner user management, creator-aware question-bank sharing, submission deletion from exam attempts, and role-filtered Recently Deleted search/restore/purge actions.

nodes.title: OEPS
nodes.summary: Online Examination and Proctoring System — full-stack web application for conducting secure online exams with proctoring

nodes.title: Backend (FastAPI)
nodes.summary: Python 3.11 FastAPI application with async SQLAlchemy ORM, JWT auth, and Alembic migrations. Serves REST API at /api/v1.

nodes.title: Frontend (Vite + TypeScript)
nodes.summary: Multi-page frontend with HTML5, TypeScript modules, and vanilla CSS. Built with Vite, served by Nginx in production. Four pages: Login, Dashboard, Exam, Result.

nodes.title: Database (PostgreSQL 15)
nodes.summary: PostgreSQL 15 Alpine container with persistent volume. Stores users, exams, questions, attempts, responses, proctoring logs, and results.

nodes.title: Docker Compose Orchestration
nodes.summary: Orchestrates 3 services: db (postgres:15-alpine), backend (FastAPI), frontend (Nginx). Backend mounts app directory for hot-reload. Frontend uses multi-stage build.

nodes.title: App Entry Point (main.py)
nodes.summary: Creates FastAPI app instance, configures CORS middleware, and mounts the v1 API router at /api/v1.

nodes.title: Settings & Configuration
nodes.summary: Pydantic BaseSettings class loading from environment variables. Supports both individual POSTGRES_* vars and single DATABASE_URL. Constructs async SQLAlchemy URI with postgresql+asyncpg scheme.

nodes.title: Security Module
nodes.summary: JWT token creation (jose), bcrypt password hashing/verification (passlib). Creates access tokens with user ID, role, and expiry.

nodes.title: API Dependencies (Auth Guards)
nodes.summary: FastAPI dependency injection for authentication. Provides get_current_user (JWT decode), get_current_active_user, get_current_active_superuser (Admin only), and get_current_active_examiner (Admin + Examiner).

nodes.title: Database Session Manager
nodes.summary: Creates async SQLAlchemy engine and AsyncSessionLocal factory. Provides get_db() async generator for dependency injection.

nodes.title: Alembic Migrations
nodes.summary: Async Alembic migration environment. Auto-generates migrations from SQLAlchemy models. Startup script auto-generates initial migration if versions/ is empty.

nodes.title: Startup Script
nodes.summary: Bash script that waits for PostgreSQL, auto-generates migrations if needed, runs alembic upgrade head, creates default superuser, then starts uvicorn with hot-reload.

nodes.title: Superuser Seeder
nodes.summary: Python script that creates default admin user (admin@example.com / password) if not exists. Uses sync-compatible asyncio loop with AsyncSessionLocal.

nodes.title: User Model
nodes.summary: SQLAlchemy model for users table. Fields: id, name, email (unique), password_hash, role (UserRole enum: ADMIN/EXAMINER/STUDENT), is_active, created_at. Relationships to exams_created, assignments, attempts, owned question folders, and shared-folder links. User snapshots now preserve password_hash for reliable trash restores.

nodes.title: Exam Model
nodes.summary: SQLAlchemy model for exams table. Fields: id, title, instructions, status (ExamStatus enum: DRAFT/SCHEDULED/LIVE/CLOSED/RESULT_PUBLISHED), start_time, duration_minutes, created_by (FK users), created_at. Relationships to creator, questions (via ExamQuestion), student assignments, teacher assignments, and attempts. Attempt relations now gate exam deletion and feed trash restore snapshots.

nodes.title: Question Model
nodes.summary: SQLAlchemy question-bank model layer. Questions store creator + folder context alongside type, prompt, options, correct_option, marks, and created_at. The same file defines question_folders and question_folder_shares for owner-managed banks, explicit examiner sharing, admin default visibility, and restore-complete question/folder snapshots with exam-link context.

nodes.title: ExamQuestion Association
nodes.summary: Many-to-many association table linking exams to questions with an order_index field. Composite PK (exam_id, question_id).

nodes.title: Assignment Model
nodes.summary: Exam assignment model layer. The file now contains both student assignments (assignments table) and teacher collaboration assignments (teacher_assignments table) so admins and examiners can control who can view and operate an exam.

nodes.title: Attempt Model
nodes.summary: SQLAlchemy model for attempts table. Fields: id, exam_id (FK), student_id (FK), status (AttemptStatus: IN_PROGRESS/SUBMITTED/EVALUATED), started_at, submitted_at. Relationships to exam, student, responses, logs, and result. Attempts can now be staff-deleted into trash and restored together with responses, logs, and results.

nodes.title: Response Model
nodes.summary: SQLAlchemy model for responses table. Stores student answers per question per attempt. Fields: id, attempt_id (FK), question_id (FK), answer, marks_awarded.

nodes.title: ProctoringLog Model
nodes.summary: SQLAlchemy model for proctoring_logs table. Logs browser events during exam. Fields: id, attempt_id (FK), event_type (TAB_SWITCH/BLUR/FOCUS), severity, metadata_info, created_at.

nodes.title: Result Model
nodes.summary: SQLAlchemy model for results table. Fields: id, attempt_id (FK, unique), total_score, max_score, published_at. One-to-one with Attempt.

nodes.title: Trash Model
nodes.summary: SQLAlchemy model for trash table. Stores soft-deleted users, exams, questions, folders, and attempts as serialized JSON snapshots. Snapshot metadata now drives role-aware listing, search, restore, and permanent-delete rules instead of relying only on the deleting user.

nodes.title: API v1 Router
nodes.summary: Central APIRouter aggregating 6 sub-routers: /auth, /users, /exams, /attempts, /reports, and /trash. Mounted at /api/v1.

nodes.title: Auth API (/auth)
nodes.summary: POST /auth/login — OAuth2 password flow. Validates email/password, returns JWT access token with user ID and role.

nodes.title: Users API (/users)
nodes.summary: Role-aware user management API. Admins keep full control, while examiners can list/search all users, create student or examiner accounts, update peer student/examiner access, toggle access for peer student/examiner accounts, and never modify self or admin access. Admin-only delete still moves users into trash, and responses include per-row capability flags for the dashboard.

nodes.title: Exams API (/exams)
nodes.summary: Exam and question-bank management API. Supports folder CRUD, question CRUD, folder sharing to examiners, creator-aware question-bank visibility (including admin default visibility for examiner-owned banks), question-bank search, exam CRUD, question-to-exam linking, collaborative teacher/student assignment, safe delete guards when submissions exist, and trash snapshots for exams, folders, and questions.

nodes.title: Attempts API (/attempts)
nodes.summary: Attempt lifecycle API. Students can start attempts only when the exam is assigned, live, and contains questions. Supports scoped attempt reads for admins/creators/assigned teachers, response auto-save, response recovery, submission, proctoring logs, live-safe response validation when questions change during an attempt, evaluation against the current exam question set, and staff-only submission deletion into trash.

nodes.title: Reports API (/reports)
nodes.summary: Advanced analytics API for admins, exam creators/collaborators, and students. GET /dashboard returns scoped portfolio KPIs, status mix, score bands, risk mix, activity timeline, and top-exam rankings. GET /exam/{exam_id}/summary and /analytics return per-exam funnels, distributions, question insights, leaderboard rows, and proctoring breakdowns. GET /attempt/{attempt_id}/analytics and /student/me power shared result analytics across all roles.

nodes.title: Trash API (/trash)
nodes.summary: Recently Deleted API for typed listing, filtering, search, restore, and permanent delete across users, exams, questions, folders, and attempts. Authorization is entity-aware: admins manage everything, while examiners only see and act on items their live delete permissions would have covered.

nodes.title: Pydantic Schemas
nodes.summary: Request/response validation schemas for users, exams, questions, attempts, tokens, and trash. Staff-facing payloads now include user capability flags, folder/question capability fields, correct_option in staff question responses, and typed trash action flags.

nodes.title: Test Suite
nodes.summary: Pytest coverage remains lightweight, but now includes a focused RBAC helper suite for user-management protections, shared question-bank access, and trash authorization. The async client fixture now degrades safely when pytest_asyncio is unavailable in local environments.

nodes.title: Login Screen
nodes.summary: Full-viewport centered card with email/password form. Submits to /api/v1/auth/login as URL-encoded form data. Stores JWT in localStorage, redirects to /dashboard.html.

nodes.title: Dashboard Screen
nodes.summary: Role-aware operations workspace with a responsive sidebar, live-refreshing exam/results/report views, examiner-accessible user management, protected admin/self access rows, folder-sharing modals, folder/question capability-driven actions, submission deletion from exam attempts, and Recently Deleted search/filter/action states driven by backend permissions.

nodes.title: Exam Screen
nodes.summary: Two-column exam workspace with question navigator, live save state, resume support, formatted countdown timer, manual/auto submission, proctoring hooks, and polling-based live exam synchronization. The screen now preserves draft answers and focus state when question wording/order/availability changes mid-attempt.

nodes.title: Result Screen
nodes.summary: Result and analytics view for a single attempt. Shows score/status plus answered-rate, time-spent, integrity breakdown, and per-question analytics. Shared attempt-report permissions let students, assigned teachers, creators, and admins open the same richer report page.

nodes.title: API Client (apiFetch)
nodes.summary: Centralized fetch wrapper. Adds Authorization Bearer token, conditionally sets JSON content type, handles 401 redirect to login, tolerates 204/text payloads, and normalizes network/API failures into clearer frontend errors.

nodes.title: Proctor Class
nodes.summary: Client-side proctoring module. Listens to visibilitychange, blur, and focus events, posts each event to the attempts log API, shows a warning flash on suspicious tab changes, and now cleans up bound listeners correctly when the exam flow ends.

nodes.title: Frontend Utilities
nodes.summary: Shared utility functions for token persistence, adaptive time formatting, debounce, toast rendering, HTML escaping, and status-badge mapping used across auth, dashboard, exam, and result flows.

nodes.title: CSS Design System
nodes.summary: Shared light-theme design system with responsive auth/dashboard/exam layouts, modern card and form primitives, and dedicated report visual components such as KPI cards, donut indicators, bar rows, timeline charts, and insight panels.

nodes.title: Nginx Reverse Proxy
nodes.summary: Serves static files from Vite build output. Proxies /api/ requests to backend:8000. Handles error pages.

nodes.title: Authentication Flow
nodes.summary: Login form → POST /api/v1/auth/login (URL-encoded) → backend validates email/password → returns JWT → stored in localStorage → subsequent API calls attach Bearer token → deps.py decodes JWT → returns User → role check guards endpoints.

nodes.title: Exam Lifecycle Flow
nodes.summary: Admin/examiner creates exams and foldered questions → question banks can be shared with examiners while admins retain default visibility → teachers and students are assigned → dashboard/exam state refreshes live without reload → student only sees assigned/eligible exams → active attempts auto-save while polling for live question changes → examiner/admin evaluates or deletes submissions into trash → exams, folders, questions, and attempts restore through Recently Deleted, while exam deletion is blocked until submissions are cleared.

nodes.title: Proctoring Flow
nodes.summary: Proctor class instantiates on exam page → listens to visibilitychange, blur, and focus → each event is POSTed to /attempts/{id}/log with metadata → backend stores the signal and later aggregates it into dashboard and per-exam analytics for event breakdowns, risk bands, and integrity alert counts.

nodes.title: Services Layer (Empty)
nodes.summary: Empty directory intended for business logic service classes. Currently all logic lives in API route handlers.

nodes.title: Repositories Layer (Empty)
nodes.summary: Empty directory intended for data access repository classes. Currently all DB queries are inline in route handlers.

edges.label: loads User by ID from token
exam/question bank creation + safe delete guards
restores attempts, responses, logs, and results

open_questions: Services and repositories directories are empty — business logic lives fully in route handlers. Should it be refactored?
Focused RBAC helper tests now exist, but end-to-end API/UI permission tests still need a DB-backed harness and async drivers in local environments.
Exam timer relies on client clock sync with server — no server-time compensation implemented.
Docker startup still logs an Alembic revision mismatch (missing revision 371f4658a015) even though the application boots.
Result screen references attempt.result but Attempt schema may not include nested result in response.

Shared folder collaborators can edit existing shared questions, but they still cannot create new questions inside another owner's folder. Confirm whether question banks should remain owner-managed or become fully collaborative.
